inline_comments.message: well personally I think that this should be pulled by the component or parent onap chart like we have in common not just here

I believe it’s more per use case basis than per component. 
But it’s a guess...
So then maybe use cases should have their own override files, no?

they're starting (one for the moment: kubernetes/onap/resources/overrides/onap-5g-network-slicing.yaml) but they'll need to cherry pick their needs, so allowing choice of subcomponents for "big" components.

I think it would be a good idea to define an override per use case
On integration side I started in F to create dedicate repository per use case
- integration/usecases/bbs
- integration/usecases/mdons

same with the simulators
so far everything was in the same repos used as a shared disk

we could then probably automate the deployment of use case lab after RC0 for the use cases based on this override + RC dockers to provide their custom lab (assuming we have enough resources...)
WIP to setup gitlab.com deployment in windriver..but I am desperately too slow.

It would avoid shared labs with risks of breaking use case A by use case B (wild update of component X), and it will track the components really used by the use cases so we could have a factual feedback on components that are rarely used by the use cases
